column13:
The first thing I want to do is thank everyone in my church family for their prayers and support.
I had an appointment on Friday with my surgeon Dr Sharkey at rothman institute and he has finally agreed to do the needed surgeries on my hip and the knee. This will involve a hip replacement on my right hip which will be the second one and a possible knee replacement on my right knee which would be the third one. He told me that because of all of my conditions and mitigating circumstances that this was going to be very hard and nothing like this has never been done before to the best of his knowledge. I told him that we are going to make history together and May end up in a medical journal. But once again I want to thank everyone for their prayers because for the longest Time it look like no one was going to touch me. The surgery should take place in the first week of March. Both surgeries will be done at the same time. He will not know about the knee until he gets in there. I really do believe in the power of family and the power of prayer. 
Please continue to pray for Brenda. That her health improves and that she can somehow find the strength to quit smoking because the doctors are saying as long as she smokes there's not much they can do for her. She is on oxygen full time which really limits her mobility. She cannot go with me to my appointments and she cannot attend many social events. Please pray that she decides to try to get better and does the exercises the doctor says she needs to do to get stronger. 
Please share this prayer request and my thank you with the my church family. 
                  Thank you

column8:
Hey Pastor 🙂
In hearing today's message i wanted to share and idea i have had for a while since ive been attending Catalyst. Ive learned that the congregation (families) have been divided amongst our deacons.  

My thought:  
To find a way to have fun smaller group "mixers /meet n greet" activities according to deacon groups. 

1) it can start us to learn to get out of our individualism (even within catalyst). 

2) getting to know others deeper that are just within my own deacon group - can help me to grow and understand others. And its part of our vision to build community.   

I think of the picnic i attended last summer.
That was a great opportunity to get to know others in the congregation socially.  I just think that we have a perfect opportunity to truly practice "building community" if we start at home - in Catalyst 1st. 
And by having some smaller based gatherings based upon deacon group, it just seems like a great place to start. 🤷🏾‍♂️

Another benefit could be that we can learn together - how to get to know others and build "effective" relationships - wthin catalyst 1st. So we can practice and give each other grace as needed while we learn Before we are truly tasked to do it  and grow catalyst from bringing in others.  😀

I just think learning how to love others, and get along with others biblically, takes practice. 😀 And we can do this with intention - to help us all grow in that area.  

Just wanted to share that thought with you to get your thoughts on the idea.

Hello Pastor Bill. As you know that I have been having Federal problems for quite some time. Way over a year. We separated for 6 months and tried to get b together before Christmas. Unfortunately it did not work brother was supposed to stay up in Pennsylvania just for the two weeks between Christmas and New years but she didn't want to come home. Is the meantime she is bouncing in and out of the hospital almost every two weeks because she continues on smoking. And I cannot stay up in that house I have tried I can't live with the smell I can't live with the smoke the dog hair the people and the mindset I just can't do it and as for myself my physical problems have gotten worse I signed the papers on August 8th to have by knee done and later on we decided to do the knee and the hip at the same time which was supposed to take place on February 4th highway to Riddle hospital to have both my hip repaired and put back in the same place and my knee put back in the right place the operation did take place on February 4th they only operated on my hip they did that I do today as they were supposed to but the thing that hurt was was that I had to go through it without Brenda by my side or even caring to be by my side she made no provisions to have the needed oxygen tanks so that she could spend a day with me in the hospital as needed and I have not seen her since I left home on February 4th she does not seem to want to talk to me will see me one of my best friends of over 50 years offered to take her to the hospital see me and she told him boy blackly that she doesn't want to see me. I feel as though I have come as far as I could go because I feel as though she doesn't want to be with me she resents me she'd rather live in a dead man's house with her grandsons then be with the bed that she's married to and quite frankly deter support now both Marley and spiritually but I'm not getting any of that. My question to you is where is the right time when you just know that they don't want you anymore and even though I love her I know that I can't be with her anymore what is the right time to walk away and regain some supplements of inner a piece.
I have been into three hospitals since I left home on February 4th I first went to riddle to get to surgery done then I got taken to find more rehab I got to move you there it took me to Paoli hospital where I have right now and this afternoon at around 11:00 I am supposed to be transferred back to Glad more we have hospital and hopefully continue me having on my hip all through this time glad that has been no contact to check with me to see how I'm doing or if I need anything she is also not made any effort to make sure that she has oxygen tax available to come see me at least once in awhile. I know this is all seeming ironic beans is Valentine's Day but I have tried to love her try to celebrate birthdays with her tried to make things work and it's just seems to be an appropriate day for me to really sit down and ask the hard questions maybe some people regardless of how much one or the other loves each other and not meant to be together. When I get out of rehab I would love to be able to be with you but what I'm going no one at my house.. anyone except you and I well we could sit down and talk this over pants man for both a man-to-man point of view and a spiritual point of view in the meantime please keep me in your prayers because as you can obviously tell I am not doing too well either physically a manly was spiritually I just listened to your sermon today on Waverly and I'm not sure that it really helped me but I am trying thank you Pastor and please keep this between you and I

Awesome message 💥💯

Your points about forgiveness and choosing not to prosecute is an interesting analogy 🤔

I have come to believe that choosing to forgive others that have wronged me actually benefits me (due to my standing with God) far more than it benefits the person i technically choose to let off the hook. Although it can be hard in the moment getting my flesh to see it that way 😃

And i feel the reverse is also true  If i choose unforgiveness solely to aim to spite the other  person that wronged me. Then the unforgiveness that im choosing is actually hurting me way worse (due to my standing with God) than I am aiming to hurt the other person by keeping them on my hook persay 

So with that cumulatuve understanding, I  have a saying which helps to simplify it for me in the moment. And the saying is  "forgive selfishly".😃

column8:
Wanted to respectfully share a thought regarding one of the application questions:
“What are you worrying about right now that reveals you don’t fully trust God’s knowledge of your situation?”

I understand the heart behind the question and agree that worry can sometimes reveal areas where we are struggling to surrender fully to Abba. I also recognize that you are speaking to Christians of different maturity levels, as well as pre-Christians who may not yet understand the nuances of statements that can sound rigid at first hearing.

I personally struggled a little with the wording because I don’t believe concern or worry always equates to not fully trusting God. As humans, we naturally feel concern for loved ones, health, and difficult circumstances. For me, the difference is what we do with those concerns. Do we hold onto them in hopelessness and self-reliance, or do we continually bring them back to Abba in trust?

My concern is that some pre-Christians or baby Christians could hear that application question and conclude that their worry automatically means they don't fully trust God.

Lately I’ve had concerns regarding both Joe and my health, yet through it all I still deeply trust that Abba has never abandoned us and never will. My concern does not come from believing He is absent, but from loving deeply while living within human weakness and limitation.

Your teaching overall was very encouraging and thought provoking. Thank you for always inviting questions and discussion.
Margarita 

P.S. My email signature is not just words to me, but how I try to live: seeking His Kingdom first, as you reminded us today.
💖 There is much I do not understand ... but my faith is ultimately in Abba's faithfulness ... TYJ 💖

Just a clarification question.  In service you answered one part of a two part question regarding verse 22, where Jesus says I don't know you to a  person that cast out a demon in his name.

The bible makes that clear in that story. 

Point to clarify in your answer: 
In Matthew 12:22-28  when the Pharisees claimed that Jesus was casting out demons under the deception of a demon, Jesus himself later spoke against why this would not be the case. 

So if Jesus made the point and exact statement to the Pharisees that demoms wouldn't cast out other demoms, then why do you think that would still be the case?
